title: Air Purifier vs Aircond: Do You Need Both?
description: Do you need an air purifier if you already have aircond in Malaysia? We explain what each machine actually does to your indoor air. Plans from RM50/month.
excerpt: Your aircond cools the air. Your air purifier cleans it. They do completely different jobs — and during haze season in Malaysia, one without the other is a half-measure.
body:
Many Malaysian households assume that if the aircond is running, the air is clean. It is a fair assumption — the room feels fresh, the temperature is comfortable, and there is a filter behind that front panel. But cooling and cleaning are two different jobs, and understanding the difference can change how you think about indoor air quality in Malaysia.

This post explains what your aircond actually does to the air, what it misses, and when adding an air purifier makes a measurable difference for your family.

## What your aircond actually does

An air conditioner circulates indoor air across a cooling coil. The refrigerant absorbs heat, the blower pushes cooled air back into the room, and the cycle repeats. Most split units have a mesh pre-filter behind the front panel that catches large dust particles and lint.

That pre-filter is not designed for fine particulate matter. It stops what you can see — hair, fabric fluff, large dust — but PM2.5 particles (the ones that penetrate deep into your lungs) pass straight through. So do volatile organic compounds (VOCs) from paint, furniture and cooking, and allergens like dust mite faeces, mould spores and pet dander.

In short, your aircond makes the room cold. It does not make the air clean.

## What an air purifier actually does

An air purifier pulls room air through multiple filter stages — typically a pre-filter, an activated carbon filter (for gases and odours), and a HEPA filter (for fine particles). H13 HEPA-grade filters, like those used in the LG PuriCare range, capture 99.97% of particles down to 0.3 microns.

That includes PM2.5 haze particles, pollen, mould spores, bacteria, dust mite allergens, and pet dander. The activated carbon layer handles cooking smells, cigarette smoke, paint fumes and VOCs.

An air purifier does not cool the room. It cleans the air at the particle level — the part your aircond skips.

## Why this matters in Malaysia

Malaysia has a unique indoor air quality challenge. We keep our windows closed most of the year because of heat and humidity, which means indoor air recirculates constantly. Add haze season (typically June to October), high humidity that encourages mould growth, and the prevalence of cooking with open flames in Malaysian kitchens, and indoor air quality can deteriorate quickly even in air-conditioned rooms.

The Department of Environment regularly records PM2.5 readings above 50 ug/m3 during haze episodes in the Klang Valley. Inside a sealed, air-conditioned room with no purifier, those particles recirculate. With an H13 HEPA purifier running, the same room can maintain PM2.5 levels below 10 ug/m3 within 30 minutes.

## The honest answer: do you need both?

**If you live in an air-conditioned home (which is most Malaysian homes), yes, you benefit from adding an air purifier.** The aircond handles temperature. The purifier handles particle and gas filtration. They complement each other — neither replaces the other.

That said, not every room needs one. Here is a practical guide:

**Bedrooms:** This is the highest-priority room. You spend 6-8 hours here with the door closed and the aircond on. A purifier ensures the air you breathe while sleeping is filtered. The [LG PuriCare AeroFurniture (RM60/month)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) doubles as a side table and covers up to 19.8 m2 — perfect for a master bedroom.

[![LG PuriCare AeroFurniture](/uploads/products/aero-furniture/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture)

**Living rooms:** If your living area is air-conditioned and you cook nearby (open-plan layouts), a purifier handles cooking odours and haze infiltration. The [LG PuriCare 360 HIT (RM50/month)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) covers 61.2 m2 with 360-degree air intake and an ionizer.

[![LG PuriCare 360 HIT](/uploads/products/puricare-360-hit/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit)

**Children's rooms and nurseries:** Young children are more vulnerable to airborne allergens and pollutants. If your child has eczema, asthma or frequent runny noses, a HEPA purifier in their room can reduce triggers. The [LG PuriCare 360 Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) includes a Baby Care mode and covers up to 104 m2.

**Home offices:** If you work from home in an air-conditioned room for 8+ hours, you are breathing recirculated air all day. A compact tower like the [AeroBooster (RM65/month)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) fits in a 24 cm space and has BAF allergy certification.

[![LG PuriCare AeroBooster](/uploads/products/aero-booster/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster)

## Can your aircond filter be upgraded instead?

Some premium aircond models offer optional HEPA or carbon filter attachments. These are better than nothing, but they have limitations. The airflow rate through an aircond is designed for cooling, not filtration — the air moves too fast for fine particle capture to be highly efficient. A dedicated purifier draws air at the right speed for its filter grade and has a sealed chamber that prevents bypass.

If your aircond has an optional ionizer or plasma filter, it helps with odours and large particles but does not match H13 HEPA performance on PM2.5.

## LG air purifiers that pair well with aircond

| Model | Coverage | From | Best for |
|---|---|---|---|
| [360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) | 61.2 m2 | RM50/mo | Living rooms, open areas |
| [AeroFurniture](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) | 19.8 m2 | RM60/mo | Bedrooms, side-table design |
| [AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) | 52.8 m2 | RM65/mo | Home offices, slim spaces |
| [AeroCatTower](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower) | - | RM70/mo | Pet owners with cats |
| [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) | 61.2 m2 | RM75/mo | Pet households, allergy sufferers |
| [Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) | 104 m2 | RM105/mo | Large homes, nurseries, multi-pet |

All models use H13 HEPA filtration and are available on LG Subscribe with free delivery and installation.

## Running cost reality check

A common concern is electricity. Most LG PuriCare models draw between 28-72 W — roughly the same as a desk fan. Running one 24 hours a day costs approximately RM15-25/month on current TNB tariffs. Compared to an aircond (which draws 800-2500 W), the purifier is a rounding error on your electricity bill.

Filter replacements are included in LG Subscribe plans. If you buy outright, expect to replace the HEPA filter every 12-24 months at RM100-200 depending on the model.

## The bottom line

Your aircond and air purifier are not competitors — they are teammates. The aircond controls temperature and humidity. The purifier removes particles, allergens, gases and odours that the aircond filter was never designed to catch.

If you already have aircond in every room and you are wondering whether an air purifier is worth the addition, the answer is yes — especially if anyone in the household has allergies, asthma or sensitivity to haze. Start with one unit in the bedroom where you sleep, and add to the living area if budget allows.

title: Best Air Purifier for Pet Owners Malaysia
description: Best air purifier for pet owners in Malaysia 2026 — we compare LG PuriCare models with V-Pet H13 filters for cat and dog dander. Plans from RM70/month.
excerpt: Pet dander, fur and odour are three different air quality problems. We compare the LG PuriCare models designed specifically for Malaysian pet households — including one that doubles as a cat tower.
body:
If you share your home with cats or dogs in Malaysia, you already know the challenges — fur on every surface, a faint pet odour that clings to soft furnishings, and for some family members, allergies that flare up when the aircond recirculates dander-laden air. A regular air purifier helps, but LG has designed a specific pet-focused range that goes further. This guide compares the three LG PuriCare models built for pet households.

## Why pet owners need a different air purifier

Standard air purifiers handle dust, pollen and haze particles well. But pet households produce three specific pollutants that require more from a purifier:

**Pet dander.** Microscopic flakes of skin shed by cats and dogs. These are the primary trigger for pet allergies. Dander particles are typically 2.5-10 microns — within HEPA capture range, but they are sticky and accumulate faster than normal household dust.

**Fur and hair.** Floating fur clogs standard pre-filters quickly, reducing airflow and filter lifespan. Pet-optimised purifiers have reinforced pre-filters designed for higher hair volume.

**Pet odour.** A combination of body oils, saliva residue and litter box gases. Standard HEPA filters do not remove gases — you need an activated carbon or deodorisation layer specifically tuned for organic compounds.

LG addresses all three with their V-Pet H13 filter system and dedicated Pet Mode across the Alpha Pet and AeroCatTower range.

## The 3 LG PuriCare models for pet owners

| Model | Type | Coverage | From | Special feature |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) | 360 tower | 61.2 m2 | RM75/mo | V-Pet H13 filter, Pet Mode |
| [Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) | Dual 360 tower | 104 m2 | RM105/mo | 2x V-Pet H13, Baby Care mode |

## Best for cat owners: LG PuriCare AeroCatTower (from RM70/month)

[![LG PuriCare AeroCatTower](/uploads/products/puricare-aero-cattower/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower)

The [AeroCatTower (AS25GCBZ0)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower) is unlike any other air purifier on the market. The top section is a heated lounging dome designed specifically for cats, with two warmth settings that your cat will gravitate to naturally. While your cat rests on top, the purifier runs below — cleaning the air of dander and fur at the source.

Cat Relax Mode reduces fan speed and noise when the dome senses your cat is resting, so the purifier does not startle them. Pet Care tracking through the LG ThinQ app monitors your cat's weight and rest time — useful data for spotting health changes early.

The 360-degree multi-stage HEPA filtration handles dander, fur and odour from the surrounding area. At RM70/month on subscription, it replaces both a cat tree and a standalone purifier — which makes it surprisingly good value for cat households that would buy both separately.

**Best for:** Households with 1-2 cats who want a combined cat tower and purifier. Not suitable for dog-only households (the dome is cat-sized).

## Best all-round pet purifier: LG PuriCare 360 Alpha Pet Single (from RM75/month)

[![LG PuriCare 360 Alpha Pet Single](/uploads/products/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single/main.png)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single)

The [Alpha Pet Single (AS65GDBY0)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) is the workhorse of LG's pet range. It covers 61.2 m2 — enough for a large living room or an open-plan living and dining area. The 360-degree V-Pet H13 filter wraps around the entire unit, pulling air from every direction.

The V-Pet filter is specifically designed for higher dander and fur load. It includes a reinforced pre-filter layer that catches floating fur before it reaches the HEPA stage, extending the HEPA filter's effective lifespan. The Clean Booster on top circulates purified air up to 7.5 metres, reaching corners that passive purifiers miss.

Pet Mode adjusts fan speed based on real-time air quality readings — ramping up when it detects elevated particulate (like after your dog shakes off after a walk) and quieting down when the air is clean. The ionizer provides additional particle capture, and UVnano sterilises the fan blades to prevent bacterial buildup.

At RM75/month for 84 months (or RM105/month for 60 months), this is the model we recommend most often to Malaysian pet owners. Noise at minimum is 26 dB — quieter than a whisper.

**Best for:** Households with cats, dogs, or both. The go-to recommendation for pet owners who want one machine to handle dander, fur and odour in their main living space.

## Best for large homes or multiple pets: LG PuriCare 360 Alpha Pet Double (from RM105/month)

[![LG PuriCare 360 Alpha Pet Double](/uploads/products/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double/main.png)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double)

The [Alpha Pet Double (AS10GDBY0)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) is the dual-stack version of the Alpha Pet Single. Two V-Pet H13 filters stacked vertically give it a CADR of 810 CMH — more than double the single unit — and coverage for rooms up to 104 m2.

If you have multiple pets, a large open-plan home, or a double-storey layout with an open staircase, this is the model that can handle the air volume. The dual Clean Booster pushes purified air across the full space, and the 4-step LED indicator (red/orange/yellow/green) gives you a visual read on real-time air quality.

The Alpha Pet Double also includes Baby Care mode — relevant for households with both pets and young children, where managing dander exposure is especially important. PM1.0 dust sensors and gas sensors provide the most granular air quality monitoring in the LG range.

At RM105/month for 84 months (or RM155/month for 60 months), it is the premium option. But for a household with 2-3 pets in a 1,500+ sq ft home, it is the only single unit that can cover the entire floor.

**Best for:** Large homes, multiple pets, households with both pets and babies, open-plan layouts.

## Quick decision guide

**I have one cat and want a combined cat tower.** The [AeroCatTower at RM70/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower) is the obvious choice.

**I have a dog, a cat, or both, and want the best single-room purifier.** The [Alpha Pet Single at RM75/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) covers 61.2 m2 with V-Pet filtration.

**I have multiple pets or a large home.** The [Alpha Pet Double at RM105/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) covers 104 m2 with dual filters and Baby Care mode.

**I do not have pets but want general air purification.** See our [Best Air Purifier Malaysia 2026](/blog/best-air-purifier-malaysia-2026/) guide instead — the 360 HIT and AeroBooster are better value for non-pet households.

## V-Pet filter vs standard H13 HEPA: what is the difference?

The V-Pet H13 filter used in the Alpha Pet range and AeroCatTower uses the same H13 HEPA media (99.97% capture at 0.3 microns) as the standard range. The difference is in the pre-filter layer and the deodorisation layer:

- **Reinforced pre-filter:** Designed for 3-4x the fur and hair load of a standard household. This protects the HEPA layer from premature clogging.
- **Enhanced deodorisation:** The carbon layer is tuned for pet-specific organic compounds — body oils, saliva proteins, litter ammonia. Standard carbon filters handle these too, but the V-Pet version has higher carbon density for faster odour neutralisation.
- **Filter replacement:** On LG Subscribe plans, V-Pet filters are replaced on the same schedule as standard filters at no extra cost. If buying outright, V-Pet replacement filters are available through LG Malaysia.

## Tips for pet owners using an air purifier

**Place it where your pet spends the most time.** If your cat sleeps on the sofa, put the purifier near the sofa. If your dog's bed is in the corner, position the unit within 3 metres. Proximity to the dander source matters more than being in the centre of the room.

**Run it 24/7 on auto mode.** Pet dander is generated continuously. Running the purifier only when you are home means dander accumulates while you are out and the machine has to work harder when you return. Auto mode adjusts fan speed based on real-time readings — it draws very little power on low.

**Clean the pre-filter monthly.** Vacuum the external pre-filter once a month to remove accumulated fur. This takes 30 seconds and keeps airflow (and purification efficiency) at peak. HEPA filter replacement is handled by your LG Subscribe plan.

**Keep bedroom doors closed during the day if needed.** If a family member has pet allergies but you want the pet to roam freely in common areas, a purifier in the bedroom with the door closed during the day creates a clean-air refuge for sleeping.

title: Best Air Purifier for Baby Nursery Malaysia
description: Best air purifier for your baby nursery in Malaysia — we compare quiet, H13 HEPA models from LG PuriCare that are safe for newborns. From RM50/month.
excerpt: Babies breathe faster than adults and their lungs are still developing. If you are setting up a nursery in Malaysia, an air purifier is not a luxury — it is basic protection against haze, dust mites and VOCs.
body:
Setting up a nursery is one of the most exciting parts of preparing for a new baby. You choose the cot, the mattress, the curtains — but the air your baby breathes is just as important as the surfaces they sleep on. In Malaysia, where haze season overlaps with the monsoon cycle and homes are sealed with aircond most of the day, a HEPA air purifier in the nursery is one of the most practical investments new parents can make.

This guide explains what to look for in a nursery air purifier, which pollutants matter most for newborns, and which LG PuriCare models are best suited for the job.

## Why babies need cleaner air than adults

Babies breathe 40-60 times per minute — roughly twice the rate of adults. Their lungs are still developing, and the air-blood barrier in their alveoli is thinner, which means airborne pollutants are absorbed more easily into their bloodstream.

Three indoor pollutants are particularly concerning for nurseries:

**PM2.5 (fine particulate matter).** During haze season, outdoor PM2.5 infiltrates even air-conditioned homes. Exposure in the first year of life is linked to increased risk of respiratory infections and reduced lung development. The World Health Organization recommends indoor PM2.5 levels below 15 ug/m3 — Malaysian homes during haze can reach 50-80 ug/m3 without filtration.

**VOCs (volatile organic compounds).** New furniture, fresh paint, cot mattresses, cleaning products and even baby wipes release VOCs. Formaldehyde from pressed wood furniture is particularly common in Malaysian homes. These gases irritate airways and are not removed by HEPA filters alone — you need an activated carbon layer.

**Dust mite allergens.** Malaysia's warm, humid climate is ideal for dust mites. Their faecal particles (10-40 microns) become airborne when bedding is disturbed and are a major trigger for eczema and wheezing in infants.

## What to look for in a nursery air purifier

**H13 HEPA filtration (minimum).** This is the grade that captures 99.97% of particles at 0.3 microns — including PM2.5, pollen, mould spores, bacteria and dust mite allergens. All LG PuriCare models use H13 HEPA.

**Activated carbon filter.** Essential for VOC and formaldehyde removal. Look for a multi-stage filter that includes a carbon or deodorisation layer alongside the HEPA.

**Low noise at minimum speed.** Babies sleep 14-17 hours a day. The purifier will run continuously in the nursery, so noise matters more here than in any other room. Look for models rated 20-26 dB at minimum speed — quieter than a library.

**No ozone emission.** Some air purifiers use ionizers or plasma that produce trace ozone. While LG's ionizer models emit ozone well below safety thresholds, some paediatricians recommend avoiding ionizers entirely for newborn rooms. Look for models where the ionizer can be switched off, or choose models without one.

**Smart monitoring.** PM2.5/PM1.0 sensors with app connectivity let you check nursery air quality from another room — useful for night monitoring without opening the door.

[![LG PuriCare 360 HIT](/uploads/products/puricare-360-hit/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit)

The 360-degree air intake means it works effectively regardless of placement. The H13 HEPA filter handles PM2.5, dust mite allergens and mould spores. The ionizer and Clean Booster circulate purified air across the room. Importantly, the ionizer can be toggled off via the remote if you prefer to run HEPA-only in the nursery.

PM10, PM2.5 and PM1.0 sensors provide real-time air quality readings on the display and through the LG ThinQ app. The 5-step fan control (Auto/Low/Mid/High/Turbo) means you can lock it on Low for overnight use.

At RM50/month, this is the entry point for nursery air purification — and honestly, for most nurseries in Malaysia, it is all you need.

[![LG PuriCare AeroFurniture](/uploads/products/aero-furniture/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture)

The [AeroFurniture (AS20GP)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) covers 19.8 m2 — which matches a typical Malaysian nursery exactly. Its side-table form factor means it replaces a bedside table next to the cot, saving floor space in a small room.

The H13 Aero Series V filter and UVnano sterilisation keep the output clean. Noise at minimum is 21 dB. The built-in Qi wireless charging pad on top is a practical bonus — you can charge your phone while doing night feeds without reaching for a cable.

Mood lighting at the base provides a soft glow that works as a nightlight — adjustable brightness and colour through the app. This is genuinely useful in a nursery where you want dim light for night checks without switching on the main light.

At RM60/month, it costs RM10 more than the 360 HIT but doubles as furniture and a nightlight. For nurseries under 20 m2, it is the most space-efficient option.

**Best for:** Small nurseries where space is tight, parents who want a built-in nightlight and wireless charging.

## Best for allergy-prone families: LG PuriCare AeroBooster (from RM65/month)

[![LG PuriCare AeroBooster](/uploads/products/aero-booster/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster)

The [AeroBooster (AS55GGWX0)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) is BAF (British Allergy Foundation) certified — an independent verification that the unit reduces airborne allergens effectively. If your family has a history of eczema, asthma or allergic rhinitis, this certification provides an extra layer of confidence.

It uses dual H13 HEPA filters in a slim 240 mm tower. The CADR of 411 CMH means it cycles the air in a 52.8 m2 room multiple times per hour. UVnano sterilises the fan blades to prevent bacterial buildup. Noise at minimum is 21 dB.

The Clean Booster on top directs purified air up to 7.5 metres — useful if the cot is across the room from where the purifier sits.

At RM65/month, it sits between the budget 360 HIT and the premium Alpha Pet Double. For parents with allergy concerns, the BAF certification makes this the strongest choice.

**Best for:** Families with allergy history, nurseries in haze-prone areas, parents who want independent allergy certification.

## Best premium option: LG PuriCare Alpha Pet Double (from RM105/month)

[![LG PuriCare Alpha Pet Double](/uploads/products/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double/main.png)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double)

The [Alpha Pet Double (AS10GDBY0)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) might seem like an odd choice for a nursery — it has "Pet" in the name. But it is the only LG model with a dedicated Baby Care mode and a PM1.0 sensor (the finest particle measurement in the range).

Baby Care mode optimises the purifier for nursery use: it prioritises ultra-fine particle capture, runs at lower noise levels, and provides enhanced air quality alerts through the app when PM1.0 readings spike. The PM1.0 sensor detects particles down to 1 micron — smaller than what PM2.5 sensors catch — giving you a more accurate read on nursery air quality.

With 104 m2 coverage and dual V-Pet H13 filters, it is overkill for the nursery alone. But if you also have pets in the house, this single unit can handle the shared living space during the day and the nursery hallway at night. The 4-step colour indicator (red/orange/yellow/green) is visible from across the room — a quick glance tells you whether air quality is good without checking the app.

At RM105/month, it is the premium option. Worth it for households that have both a baby and pets, or parents who want the most advanced air quality monitoring available.

**Best for:** Households with pets and a baby, parents who want PM1.0 monitoring and Baby Care mode.

## Setting up your nursery air purifier

**Place it at least 1 metre from the cot.** Direct airflow on a sleeping baby can disturb their sleep and dry out their skin. Position the purifier across the room or to the side.

**Run it 24/7 on auto or low.** Nursery air should be continuously filtered, not just cleaned when you think of it. Auto mode adjusts based on real-time readings. If your baby is a light sleeper, lock it on the lowest manual setting overnight.

**Close the nursery door.** A sealed room with a purifier creates a clean-air zone. If the door is open, the purifier has to work against the rest of the house.

**Replace filters on schedule.** On LG Subscribe, this is handled for you — filters arrive on schedule or a technician visits depending on your plan. If you bought outright, set a calendar reminder. A clogged filter is worse than no filter.

**Air out the room before baby arrives.** New furniture, paint and flooring off-gas the most in the first 2-4 weeks. If possible, set up the nursery and run the purifier for at least 2 weeks before your baby sleeps there. Open windows during the day if outdoor air quality permits, and let the purifier handle the remainder.

title: HEPA Filters Explained: H13 vs True HEPA
description: H13 HEPA vs True HEPA filters explained for Malaysian buyers. Learn what filter grades mean, why H13 matters for haze, and which LG models use it.
excerpt: Not all HEPA filters are equal. H13 captures 99.97% of particles at 0.3 microns, while 'True HEPA' is a marketing term with no fixed standard. Here is what the grades actually mean for Malaysian air quality.
body:
If you are shopping for an air purifier in Malaysia, you have probably seen the terms H13 HEPA, True HEPA and HEPA-type on product listings. They sound similar, but the differences matter — especially during haze season when PM2.5 levels in the Klang Valley can spike above 100 ug/m3 within hours. This post explains what each HEPA grade actually means and why it should influence your buying decision.

## What HEPA stands for

HEPA stands for High Efficiency Particulate Air. It is a filtration standard, not a brand name. The standard was originally developed in the 1940s for nuclear research facilities that needed to capture radioactive dust particles from the air.

Today, HEPA filters are graded by the European standard EN 1822, which classifies them from E10 (the lowest efficiency) through H14 (the highest commonly used in consumer and medical applications). The grade tells you two things: the minimum particle size the filter can capture, and the percentage of those particles it stops.

## The HEPA grades that matter for home use

| Grade | Efficiency at 0.3 microns | Typical use |
|---|---|---|
| E11 | 95% | Basic industrial filtration |
| E12 | 99.5% | Some budget air purifiers |
| H13 | 99.97% | Premium air purifiers, hospitals |
| H14 | 99.995% | Clean rooms, surgical theatres |

The jump from E12 to H13 is significant. An E12 filter lets through 5 out of every 1,000 particles at the most penetrating particle size (0.3 microns). An H13 filter lets through only 0.3 out of every 1,000. In a room with heavy haze infiltration, that difference translates to measurably cleaner air.

H14 is overkill for residential use. The filter media is denser, which increases resistance and requires more powerful fans — driving up noise and energy consumption. H13 is the sweet spot for home air purifiers.

## What is "True HEPA" then

Here is where the confusion starts. "True HEPA" is not an official grade under EN 1822 or any other international standard. It is a marketing term used primarily in North America to distinguish filters that meet or exceed H13-equivalent performance from cheaper "HEPA-type" or "HEPA-style" filters that do not.

When a manufacturer labels a filter "True HEPA," they are usually claiming it meets the US DOE standard of 99.97% efficiency at 0.3 microns — which is functionally equivalent to H13. But because "True HEPA" has no legally enforced definition in Malaysia, some brands use it loosely. A filter labelled "True HEPA" might be H13-equivalent, or it might be E12 with optimistic marketing.

The safest approach: look for the explicit H13 grade. If the product page or spec sheet does not state H13 (or EN 1822 compliance), assume it is not.

## What is "HEPA-type" or "HEPA-style"

These terms are red flags. A "HEPA-type" filter looks like a HEPA filter — it has pleated media in a frame — but it does not meet any recognised HEPA efficiency standard. Typical efficiency is 85-95% at 0.3 microns, which means it lets through 50 to 150 particles for every 1,000. That is adequate for large dust particles but insufficient for PM2.5 haze particulates.

If your primary concern is haze season protection, cooking fume particles, or allergen reduction, a HEPA-type filter will not deliver the results you expect.

## Why 0.3 microns is the benchmark

You might wonder why HEPA efficiency is measured at 0.3 microns specifically. This size is called the Most Penetrating Particle Size (MPPS). Particles larger than 0.3 microns are easier to catch because they collide with filter fibres through inertial impaction. Particles smaller than 0.3 microns are easier to catch because they move erratically (Brownian motion) and stick to fibres through diffusion.

At 0.3 microns, neither mechanism is dominant — the particle is too small for reliable inertial capture and too large for efficient diffusion. This is the hardest size for any mechanical filter to catch. If a filter achieves 99.97% efficiency at 0.3 microns, it performs even better at all other sizes.

For context, PM2.5 haze particles range from 0.1 to 2.5 microns. An H13 filter handles the full range, including the most difficult 0.3-micron fraction.

## How H13 applies to Malaysian air quality

Malaysia faces three recurring indoor air quality challenges, and H13 HEPA addresses all three.

**Haze season (June to October).** Transboundary haze from agricultural burning pushes PM2.5 levels into the unhealthy range across the Klang Valley, Johor and Sarawak. H13 filtration reduces indoor PM2.5 from 50-80 ug/m3 to below 10 ug/m3 within 30 minutes in a sealed room.

**Year-round dust and allergens.** Malaysia's humidity promotes dust mite populations. Their allergenic faecal particles (2.5-10 microns) are well within H13 capture range. Mould spores (2-20 microns) and pollen (10-100 microns) are captured with even greater efficiency.

**Cooking fumes in open-plan homes.** Fine oil droplets from wok cooking are typically 0.5-5 microns. H13 captures these particles effectively, though an activated carbon layer is also needed to handle the gaseous odour component.

## LG PuriCare models that use H13 HEPA

Every air purifier in the LG PuriCare range uses H13-grade HEPA filtration as a minimum. Here is a summary.

| Model | Filter type | Coverage | From |
|---|---|---|---|
| [360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) | H13 HEPA + Deodorising | 61.2 m2 | RM50/mo |
| [AeroFurniture](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) | H13 HEPA + UVnano | 19.8 m2 | RM60/mo |
| [AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) | Dual H13 HEPA + UVnano | 52.8 m2 | RM65/mo |
| [AeroCatTower](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower) | H13 HEPA | — | RM70/mo |
| [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) | V-Pet H13 HEPA | 61.2 m2 | RM75/mo |
| [Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) | 2x V-Pet H13 HEPA | 104 m2 | RM105/mo |

The [AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) is also BAF (British Allergy Foundation) certified, which provides independent third-party verification that the unit reduces airborne allergens effectively. If you are choosing between models based on filtration credibility, the BAF certification is a concrete differentiator.

[![LG PuriCare AeroBooster](/uploads/products/aero-booster/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster)

## How to verify a filter is genuinely H13

Three checks you can do before buying any air purifier, regardless of brand.

**Check the spec sheet for EN 1822 compliance.** The product datasheet should explicitly state H13 or reference EN 1822. If it says only "HEPA" or "True HEPA" without a grade, ask the retailer for the test report.

**Look for third-party certification.** BAF certification, AHAM Verified, or Allergy UK approval all require independent lab testing. These logos are harder to fake than a "True HEPA" label.

**Check the CADR rating.** Clean Air Delivery Rate is measured by independent labs and published on the spec sheet. A genuine H13 purifier with a high CADR confirms that the filter and fan system work together efficiently — not just that the filter media is good.

## Filter replacement and cost

H13 filters are denser than lower-grade filters, which means they accumulate particles faster and need replacement on schedule. Most LG PuriCare models recommend filter replacement every 12-24 months depending on usage and air quality conditions.

On LG Subscribe plans, filter replacements are included in your monthly subscription — shipped to your door on a Self-Service plan or replaced by a technician on a Regular Visit plan. If you buy the purifier outright, expect to pay RM100-250 per replacement filter depending on the model.

Skipping a filter change is worse than using a lower-grade filter. A clogged H13 filter restricts airflow, forces the motor to work harder, and can actually reduce purification efficiency below what a fresh E12 filter would deliver. Stay on schedule.

## The bottom line

H13 is the standard that matters for Malaysian households. It captures 99.97% of particles at the most difficult size, handles haze PM2.5, allergens and cooking fumes, and is the grade used in hospitals and medical facilities. "True HEPA" usually means the same thing but lacks a legally enforced definition. "HEPA-type" means something significantly weaker.

When comparing air purifiers, look for the H13 label, check for third-party certification, and ignore marketing language that does not reference a specific grade.

title: Air Purifier Running Cost Malaysia
description: Real electricity costs for running an air purifier in Malaysia. We calculate monthly TNB bills for 6 LG models so you know what to budget. From RM5/month.
excerpt: An air purifier uses about the same electricity as a desk fan. We calculate the real monthly TNB cost for each LG PuriCare model so you can budget accurately — spoiler: it is under RM25 for most units.
body:
One of the most common questions Malaysian buyers ask before subscribing to an air purifier is how much it adds to the electricity bill. The answer is reassuring — most air purifiers draw between 5W and 70W, which puts them in the same category as a desk fan or a phone charger. This post calculates the real monthly TNB cost for each LG PuriCare air purifier so you can budget with confidence.

## How we calculated the costs

We used TNB's 2026 residential tariff of 44.43 sen per kWh for consumption above 600 kWh/month, which applies to most Malaysian households running aircond. For lower-usage households (under 600 kWh), the effective rate is lower, so the costs below represent the upper end.

The formula is straightforward: Wattage x Hours per day x 30 days / 1000 x RM0.4443 = Monthly electricity cost.

We calculated two scenarios: running the purifier 12 hours per day (daytime only or bedroom overnight) and 24 hours per day (recommended during haze season).

## Monthly electricity cost by LG PuriCare model

| Model | Wattage (low/high) | 12 hrs/day | 24 hrs/day |
|---|---|---|---|
| [360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) | 5W / 56W | RM0.80–RM9 | RM1.60–RM18 |
| [AeroFurniture](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) | 5W / 40W | RM0.80–RM6.40 | RM1.60–RM12.80 |
| [AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) | 5W / 55W | RM0.80–RM8.80 | RM1.60–RM17.60 |
| [AeroCatTower](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower) | 6W / 42W | RM0.96–RM6.70 | RM1.92–RM13.40 |
| [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) | 6W / 58W | RM0.96–RM9.30 | RM1.92–RM18.50 |
| [Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) | 8W / 72W | RM1.28–RM11.50 | RM2.56–RM23 |

The low figure represents the purifier running on its lowest fan speed (sleep mode or auto mode in clean air). The high figure represents running on maximum fan speed continuously, which only happens during heavy haze or when you first turn the unit on in a dusty room.

## Realistic monthly cost: RM5-15 for most households

In practice, most LG PuriCare models spend 80-90% of their operating time on low or auto mode. The smart sensor detects current air quality and adjusts the fan speed accordingly. On a normal day with windows closed and aircond running, the sensor reads clean air within 10-20 minutes and drops to the lowest speed.

A realistic monthly electricity cost for most households is RM5-15 — depending on the model and how many hours per day you run it. During haze season, expect the higher end as the sensor ramps up fan speed more frequently.

## How this compares to other appliances

To put the cost in perspective, here is what other common household appliances cost to run per month under the same TNB tariff.

| Appliance | Typical wattage | Monthly cost (typical use) |
|---|---|---|
| Air purifier (auto mode) | 5-30W | RM5-15 |
| Desk fan | 35-50W | RM8-15 |
| Ceiling fan | 60-75W | RM12-20 |
| LED TV (65-77 inch) | 80-150W | RM15-30 |
| Air conditioner (1.5HP inverter) | 800-1200W | RM100-160 |
| Air conditioner (1.5HP non-inverter) | 1200-1800W | RM180-250 |
| Refrigerator (inverter) | 30-60W avg | RM30-50 |

An air purifier costs roughly the same as running a desk fan. It is a fraction of what your aircond costs — and running both together gives you cooled air that is also clean.

## Air purifier vs aircond: the electricity gap

The most common objection we hear is "I already run aircond, I do not want another appliance adding to my bill." The reality is that an air purifier adds RM5-15 to your monthly TNB bill, while your aircond adds RM100-250. The purifier is a rounding error on your electricity bill.

If you are concerned about overall electricity costs, the highest-impact move is switching from a non-inverter to an [inverter aircond](/products/air-conditioners). That single change saves RM50-100 per month — enough to cover the air purifier subscription and its electricity cost combined.

## Tips to minimise running cost

**Use auto mode.** Let the smart sensor manage fan speed. Manual high mode wastes electricity when the air is already clean.

**Keep the room sealed.** Close doors and windows when the purifier is running. An open door forces the purifier to work against a constant inflow of unfiltered air, keeping the fan on higher speeds for longer.

**Place the purifier correctly.** Do not push it against a wall or block the air intake. Restricted airflow forces the motor to work harder, drawing more power for less effective filtration. Leave at least 30 cm of clearance around the unit.

**Replace filters on schedule.** A clogged filter increases motor load. On LG Subscribe, filter replacements are included in your monthly plan. If you bought outright, follow the recommended schedule.

**Run it 24/7 on auto during haze.** This sounds counterintuitive for saving electricity, but auto mode at low speed overnight draws only 5-8W — less than a nightlight. Turning the purifier off overnight and restarting in the morning forces it to run on high for 20-30 minutes to clear accumulated particles, which uses more total energy.

## The total cost of clean air

Let us put the full picture together for the most popular model, the [360 HIT at RM50/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit).

| Cost item | Monthly |
|---|---|
| LG Subscribe plan | RM50 |
| Estimated electricity (auto, 24/7) | ~RM8 |
| Filter replacements | Included |
| Maintenance | Included |
| **Total** | **~RM58/month** |

For RM58/month — less than a moderate restaurant meal for two — you get H13 HEPA filtration covering 61.2 m2, scheduled filter changes, full warranty, and air quality you can verify on your phone through the LG ThinQ app.

## Related reading

- [TNB Tariff 2026: What It Means for You](/blog/tnb-tariff-2026-what-new-rates-mean-for-home/)
- [Best Air Purifier Malaysia 2026](/blog/best-air-purifier-malaysia-2026/)
- [Air Purifier vs Aircond: Do You Need Both?](/blog/air-purifier-vs-aircond-do-you-need-both/)

## Next step

Want to know the exact running cost for a specific model in your home? Message us on WhatsApp with the model you are considering and your average daily usage hours. We will calculate the monthly electricity cost for your situation.

title: LG vs Dyson vs Xiaomi Air Purifier Malaysia
description: LG vs Dyson vs Xiaomi air purifier compared for Malaysian homes. Filtration, noise, price, maintenance and smart features side by side. Full 2026 guide.
excerpt: Three brands dominate air purifier searches in Malaysia — LG, Dyson and Xiaomi. We compare them on filtration, noise, price, maintenance and smart features so you can decide which one fits your home.
body:
LG, Dyson and Xiaomi are the three air purifier brands that Malaysian buyers search for most often. Each takes a different approach to filtration, pricing and design, and each has clear strengths. This comparison lays out the differences across the factors that matter most — so you can choose the brand and model that fits your home, your budget and your indoor air quality needs.

## The three brands at a glance

**LG PuriCare** focuses on HEPA filtration with smart sensors, and is the only brand among the three that offers a full subscription model in Malaysia — no upfront cost, maintenance included, filters replaced on schedule.

**Dyson** combines air purification with bladeless fan technology and, in some models, heating. The design is distinctive and the engineering is refined. But the price point is high, and filter replacements are entirely on you.

**Xiaomi** targets the budget end of the market with simple, effective HEPA purifiers controlled through the Mi Home app. The upfront cost is low, but the feature set and build quality reflect the price.

## Filtration compared

| Feature | LG PuriCare | Dyson | Xiaomi |
|---|---|---|---|
| Filter grade | H13 HEPA (all models) | H13 HEPA (most models) | H13 HEPA (select models), E12 (budget) |
| Carbon filter | Yes (deodorising layer) | Yes (activated carbon) | Yes (activated carbon on most) |
| UVnano sterilisation | Yes (AeroBooster, AeroFurniture) | No | No |
| BAF allergy certified | Yes (AeroBooster) | Yes (select models) | No |
| Ioniser | Yes (360 HIT, Alpha Pet) | No | No |

All three brands offer H13 HEPA on their mid-range and premium models. The difference is in the secondary filtration layers. LG adds UVnano sterilisation on the AeroBooster and AeroFurniture, which kills bacteria on the fan blades and air outlet — something neither Dyson nor Xiaomi offers. Dyson's sealed filtration system is well-engineered, with the entire airpath designed to prevent leaks around the filter. Xiaomi keeps things simple — a cylindrical HEPA-carbon combo filter that slots into the base.

For Malaysian haze protection, all three brands at the H13 level will handle PM2.5 effectively. The LG and Dyson models offer more advanced secondary filtration, while Xiaomi delivers the basics at a lower cost.

## Coverage and CADR

| Model | CADR (approx.) | Coverage | Price |
|---|---|---|---|
| [LG 360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) | ~380 CMH | 61.2 m2 | RM50/mo |
| [LG AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) | ~411 CMH | 52.8 m2 | RM65/mo |
| Dyson TP07 | ~290 CMH | ~35 m2 | ~RM2,800 outright |
| Dyson HP07 | ~290 CMH | ~35 m2 | ~RM3,500 outright |
| Xiaomi 4 Pro | ~500 CMH | ~60 m2 | ~RM700 outright |
| Xiaomi 4 Lite | ~360 CMH | ~43 m2 | ~RM400 outright |

Xiaomi leads on raw CADR per ringgit — the Xiaomi 4 Pro moves more air than any unit in this comparison and costs a fraction of the others. However, CADR alone does not determine real-world performance. Noise at high speed, filter seal quality, and sensor accuracy all affect how well the purifier performs in daily use.

LG and Dyson offer better room coverage consistency because their sealed airpaths and multi-directional air distribution ensure purified air reaches all corners. Xiaomi's top-discharge design is effective but can leave dead zones in larger rooms.

## Noise levels

| Model | Minimum noise | Maximum noise |
|---|---|---|
| LG 360 HIT | 20 dB | ~52 dB |
| LG AeroBooster | 21 dB | ~52 dB |
| Dyson TP07 | ~30 dB | ~62 dB |
| Xiaomi 4 Pro | ~30 dB | ~64 dB |

LG wins on minimum noise. The 360 HIT at 20 dB and the AeroBooster at 21 dB are quieter than a whisper — suitable for light sleepers and nurseries. Dyson and Xiaomi are louder at minimum speed, and significantly louder at maximum. If the purifier is going in a bedroom, this difference matters every night.

## Price and total cost of ownership

This is where the comparison gets interesting. The three brands use completely different pricing models.

**Xiaomi: lowest upfront, maintenance on you.** A Xiaomi 4 Pro costs approximately RM700 outright. Replacement filters cost RM100-150 each and should be changed every 6-12 months. Over 5 years: ~RM700 + RM750-1,500 in filters = RM1,450-2,200 total.

**Dyson: high upfront, maintenance on you.** A Dyson TP07 costs approximately RM2,800 outright. Replacement filters cost RM250-350 each and should be changed every 12 months. Over 5 years: ~RM2,800 + RM1,250-1,750 in filters = RM4,050-4,550 total.

**LG Subscribe: no upfront, maintenance included.** The [LG 360 HIT at RM50/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) over 84 months = RM4,200 total, with every filter replacement, warranty claim and maintenance visit included. The [AeroBooster at RM65/month](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) over 84 months = RM5,460 total with the same inclusions.

| Brand/Model | 5-year total cost | Filters included | Warranty |
|---|---|---|---|
| Xiaomi 4 Pro | RM1,450–2,200 | No | 1 year |
| Dyson TP07 | RM4,050–4,550 | No | 2 years |
| LG 360 HIT (Subscribe) | RM3,000 (60 mo) | Yes | Full term |
| LG AeroBooster (Subscribe) | RM3,900 (60 mo) | Yes | Full term |

Xiaomi is the cheapest option by a wide margin. Dyson is the most expensive. LG Subscribe sits in between but includes maintenance and warranty for the full term — which removes the ongoing admin and cost uncertainty.

## Smart features

| Feature | LG (ThinQ app) | Dyson (MyDyson app) | Xiaomi (Mi Home app) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Real-time PM2.5 display | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| PM1.0 sensor | Yes (Alpha Pet Double) | No | No |
| VOC/gas sensor | Yes (select models) | Yes | No |
| Auto mode (sensor-driven) | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Schedule/timer | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Voice control (Alexa/Google) | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Air quality history | Yes | Yes | Yes |

All three apps are functional. Dyson's app provides the most detailed historical air quality charts. LG's ThinQ app integrates with other LG appliances if you have a connected home. Xiaomi's Mi Home app is the most ecosystem-friendly if you already use Xiaomi smart home devices.

## Maintenance and filter replacement

This is where LG Subscribe creates the clearest differentiation.

**On LG Subscribe:** filters are replaced on schedule at no extra cost. On a Self-Service plan, replacement filters are shipped to your door. On a Regular Visit plan, a technician handles everything. You do not need to remember, order, or pay for filters separately.

**On Dyson and Xiaomi:** you buy replacement filters yourself, track the replacement schedule yourself, and install them yourself. If you forget or delay, purification efficiency drops. Neither brand offers a subscription or maintenance service in Malaysia.

For buyers who are disciplined about maintenance, this is a non-issue. For the majority of households where filter replacement gets forgotten after the first year, LG Subscribe removes that failure point entirely.

## Build quality and design

Dyson leads on industrial design. The bladeless fan format is visually distinctive and doubles as a room fan. The build quality — machined metal, sealed construction, precise tolerances — reflects the price premium.

LG offers a range of form factors: the [AeroFurniture](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) doubles as a side table with wireless charging, the [AeroCatTower](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-aero-cattower) integrates a heated cat dome, and the AeroBooster fits in a 240 mm wide slot. Build quality is solid across the range.

Xiaomi's build is functional. Plastic body, simple cylindrical design, lightweight. It does the job but does not make a design statement.

## Which brand should you choose

**Choose Xiaomi if** your priority is the lowest total cost, you are comfortable managing filter replacements yourself, and you want a simple purifier that handles haze season without extra features. The Xiaomi 4 Pro is hard to beat on value.

**Choose Dyson if** you want a purifier that doubles as a fan (or heater), you value design and build quality above all else, and you are prepared to pay the premium for both the unit and ongoing filter costs.

**Choose LG if** you want H13 HEPA filtration with included maintenance and no upfront cost. LG Subscribe is the only option among the three that bundles filters, warranty and servicing into one monthly payment. title: Air Purifier for Asthma and Allergies
description: How air purifiers help with asthma and allergies in Malaysia. The science behind HEPA filtration for dust mites, haze and pet dander. BAF-certified picks.
excerpt: Dust mites, haze and pet dander trigger asthma and allergies in millions of Malaysians. Here is what the science says about HEPA air purifiers — and which models carry independent allergy certification.
body:
Asthma and allergic rhinitis affect a significant portion of the Malaysian population. The National Health and Morbidity Survey estimates that around 10-15% of Malaysian adults and up to 20% of children experience some form of allergic respiratory condition. In a tropical climate where dust mites thrive, haze season recurs annually, and indoor air recirculates through aircond systems for hours each day, managing indoor air quality is not optional — it is part of the treatment plan.

This post covers the science behind how HEPA air purifiers reduce asthma and allergy triggers, what to look for in a purifier, and which LG models carry independent allergy certification.

## Common indoor allergy triggers in Malaysia

Understanding what triggers your symptoms is the first step to managing them. In Malaysian homes, four airborne allergens dominate.

**Dust mite allergens.** Malaysia's warm, humid climate (25-35 degrees Celsius, 70-90% relative humidity) is ideal for dust mite populations. The allergen is not the mite itself but its faecal pellets — tiny particles of 10-40 microns that become airborne when bedding, cushions or carpets are disturbed. These particles are the number one indoor allergy trigger in Southeast Asia.

**Haze particulates (PM2.5).** During haze season (typically June to October), fine particulate matter from agricultural burning infiltrates indoor spaces even with windows closed. PM2.5 particles inflame airways, trigger bronchospasm in asthma sufferers, and worsen nasal congestion in allergic rhinitis patients.

**Pet dander.** Microscopic flakes of skin shed by cats and dogs. Cat dander is particularly problematic because the particles are smaller (1-5 microns) and remain airborne for hours. Even in homes without pets, cat dander is often present on visitors' clothing.

**Mould spores.** High humidity in Malaysian bathrooms, kitchens and poorly ventilated rooms encourages mould growth. Mould spores (2-20 microns) trigger both allergic reactions and, in severe cases, hypersensitivity pneumonitis.

## How HEPA filtration helps

A HEPA air purifier works by forcing air through a dense mat of randomly arranged fibres. Particles are captured through three mechanisms: interception (medium particles follow the airstream and touch a fibre), impaction (large particles cannot follow the airstream curve and collide with fibres), and diffusion (the smallest particles move erratically and stick to fibres).

H13-grade HEPA filters capture 99.97% of particles at 0.3 microns — the most penetrating particle size. This means they are effective against all four major Malaysian indoor allergens.

| Allergen | Particle size | Captured by H13 HEPA |
|---|---|---|
| Dust mite faecal pellets | 10-40 microns | Yes (with high efficiency) |
| Haze PM2.5 | 0.1-2.5 microns | Yes |
| Cat dander | 1-5 microns | Yes |
| Dog dander | 5-10 microns | Yes |
| Mould spores | 2-20 microns | Yes |
| Pollen | 10-100 microns | Yes (with high efficiency) |

## What the research says

Multiple peer-reviewed studies have shown that HEPA air purifiers reduce airborne allergen concentrations and improve symptoms in asthma and allergy patients.

A 2020 systematic review published in the Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology found that portable HEPA air purifiers reduced indoor PM2.5 concentrations by 25-50% and improved peak expiratory flow rates in asthmatic children. The effect was strongest when the purifier was placed in the bedroom and run continuously overnight.

A separate study in Annals of Allergy, Asthma and Immunology found that HEPA filtration combined with mattress encasings reduced dust mite allergen levels in bedrooms by 60-80% over a 12-month period.

The evidence is consistent: HEPA filtration is not a cure for asthma or allergies, but it meaningfully reduces the allergen burden that triggers symptoms.

## What to look for in a purifier for asthma and allergies

**H13 HEPA minimum.** Lower-grade filters (E12, HEPA-type) let through too many fine particles. For allergy management, H13 is the practical minimum. See our [HEPA filter guide](/blog/hepa-filter-h13-true-hepa-explained/) for a detailed breakdown of filter grades.

**Independent allergy certification.** Look for BAF (British Allergy Foundation) or Allergy UK certification. These require independent lab testing to verify that the purifier reduces airborne allergens — not just particles in general. Marketing claims like "anti-allergen" without certification are unverifiable.

**Adequate room coverage.** An undersized purifier will not cycle the air frequently enough to maintain low allergen levels. Match the CADR and coverage rating to your room size. For bedrooms (10-20 m2), even the entry-level models are sufficient. For living rooms (30-60 m2), choose a unit rated for the full area.

**Activated carbon layer.** While HEPA captures particles, activated carbon adsorbs gases and VOCs. This is relevant for allergy sufferers who also react to chemical irritants from cleaning products, new furniture or cooking fumes.

**Low noise.** If the purifier is running in a bedroom overnight (which it should be for allergy management), noise matters. Models under 25 dB on sleep mode will not disturb your rest.

## LG models with allergy credentials

[![LG PuriCare AeroBooster — BAF Certified](/uploads/products/aero-booster/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster)

The [LG PuriCare AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) is the standout choice for allergy sufferers. It is BAF (British Allergy Foundation) certified, uses dual H13 HEPA filters, and includes UVnano sterilisation that eliminates bacteria on the fan blades. At 21 dB minimum noise and 52.8 m2 coverage, it is quiet enough for a bedroom and powerful enough for a living room. The subscription starts at RM65/month.

The [360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) at RM50/month is a strong alternative if budget is the priority. It covers 61.2 m2 with H13 HEPA and runs at 20 dB minimum — the quietest in the LG range. It does not carry BAF certification but uses the same H13 filter grade.

For pet allergy sufferers, the [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) at RM75/month uses the V-Pet H13 filter optimised for dander and fur, covering 61.2 m2. The [Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) at RM105/month extends coverage to 104 m2 and adds Baby Care mode for households with both pets and young children.

## Placement tips for allergy management

**Bedroom is the priority.** You spend 6-8 hours in the bedroom with the door closed. Place the purifier in this room first. If you can only afford one unit, this is where it goes.

**Keep the door closed.** A sealed room with a running purifier creates a clean-air zone. Open the door only briefly when entering or leaving. The purifier will recover air quality within minutes.

**Run it continuously on auto mode.** Allergens are generated and released constantly — by bedding movement, foot traffic, pet activity, and outdoor infiltration. Running the purifier only when symptoms flare means allergens have already accumulated to triggering levels.

**Position it away from walls.** Leave at least 30 cm clearance around the unit for unobstructed airflow. Pushing it against a wall reduces circulation efficiency.

**Combine with other measures.** An air purifier reduces airborne allergens but does not eliminate them from surfaces. Combine HEPA filtration with dust mite-proof mattress covers, weekly hot-water bedding washes (above 60 degrees Celsius), and regular vacuuming with a HEPA-equipped vacuum.

## A note on medical advice

Air purifiers are a useful environmental control measure, but they are not a substitute for medical treatment. If you have asthma or severe allergies, follow your doctor's prescribed medication plan. An air purifier complements your treatment by reducing the triggers that cause flare-ups — it does not replace inhalers, antihistamines or immunotherapy.

title: Where to Place Your Air Purifier
description: Air purifier placement guide for Malaysian homes. Learn the correct distance from walls, height, and room-by-room positioning to get the most from your unit.
excerpt: Where you place your air purifier affects how well it cleans the air. This room-by-room guide covers the correct distance from walls, height, and common mistakes that reduce purification efficiency.
body:
You bought an air purifier, plugged it in, and placed it wherever it fits. That is what most people do, and it is one of the most common reasons an air purifier underperforms. Placement directly affects how efficiently the unit cycles and cleans the air in your room. This guide covers the principles of correct placement and applies them room by room for a typical Malaysian home.

## The three rules of air purifier placement

Regardless of the brand or model, three basic rules apply to every air purifier.

**Rule 1: Leave clearance around the unit.** Most air purifiers draw air in from the sides or bottom and push clean air out from the top. If the intake is blocked — by a wall, curtain, sofa or cabinet — airflow drops and the motor works harder to pull in less air. Leave at least 30 cm of clearance on all sides of the unit.

For 360-degree intake models like the [LG 360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) and [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single), this is especially important. These units pull air from every direction, so pushing them into a corner blocks half the intake area.

**Rule 2: Place it in the room you use most.** If you have one purifier, put it in the room where you spend the most hours. For most Malaysian families, this is the living room during the day and the bedroom at night. Moving the purifier between rooms is practical — LG PuriCare floor-standing models weigh 8-15 kg and have handles or smooth bases for repositioning.

**Rule 3: Keep doors and windows closed.** An air purifier in a room with an open door is working against a constant inflow of unfiltered air from the rest of the house. Close the door, let the purifier clean the room's air volume, and the sensor will show green within 15-30 minutes.

## Room-by-room placement guide

### Living room

The living room is typically the largest room in a Malaysian home (20-50 m2) and the area where the family gathers during the day. Place the purifier in an open area — not behind the sofa, not inside a TV cabinet, and not wedged between the wall and a bookshelf.

Ideal position: near the centre of the longest wall, with at least 30 cm clearance on all sides. If you have an open-plan living and dining area, position the purifier closer to the seating area where people spend the most time.

For living rooms, the [LG 360 HIT (61.2 m2, RM50/mo)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) or [AeroBooster (52.8 m2, RM65/mo)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) are the right capacity. The AeroBooster's slim 240 mm width fits into tight spots without sacrificing intake clearance.

[![LG PuriCare AeroBooster — slim enough for tight living room corners](/uploads/products/aero-booster/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster)

### Bedroom

The bedroom is where placement matters most because you spend 6-8 consecutive hours here. Place the purifier at least 1.5 metres from the head of the bed. Direct airflow on your face while sleeping can dry out your nasal passages and skin — counterproductive if you are using the purifier for allergy management.

Ideal position: on the opposite wall from the bed, or beside the wardrobe. Avoid placing it on the floor directly next to the bed where it might be kicked or blocked by fallen pillows.

The [AeroFurniture (19.8 m2, RM60/mo)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture) is designed specifically for bedrooms — its side-table form factor means it replaces a bedside table at a comfortable distance from the bed. The Qi wireless charging pad on top keeps your phone charged overnight.

[![LG PuriCare AeroFurniture — doubles as a bedroom side table](/uploads/products/aero-furniture/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/aero-furniture)

### Children's room or nursery

Place the purifier at least 1 metre from the cot or bed. Children are lighter sleepers and more sensitive to direct airflow. Position it across the room on a clear section of floor.

Avoid placing it on high furniture in a child's room — a toddler pulling on the unit is a safety risk. Floor-standing models are more stable. Make sure the power cord is secured against the wall and not trailing across the floor.

The [360 HIT (RM50/mo)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) is the quietest option at 20 dB minimum — it will not disturb a sleeping child.

### Home office

If you work from home in an air-conditioned room for 8 or more hours, you are breathing recirculated air all day. Place the purifier within 2-3 metres of your desk, but not directly beside your monitor where the airflow creates a draught across your workspace.

Ideal position: beside or behind your desk, with the clean air output directed into the room rather than straight at your face.

The [AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) at 240 mm wide fits beside a desk without taking floor space. Its Clean Booster directs purified air up to 8.5 metres, covering a standard home office from a corner position.

### Open-plan kitchen and living area

Malaysian open-plan homes present a unique challenge: cooking fumes from the kitchen travel directly into the living area. Place the purifier between the kitchen and the living seating area — it will intercept cooking particles before they settle on your sofa and curtains.

Do not place the purifier next to the stove or hob. Grease, steam and high temperatures near the cooking surface will damage the filter and clog the intake faster. A distance of at least 2 metres from the cooking surface is recommended.

For open-plan layouts, the [Alpha Pet Double (104 m2, RM105/mo)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) has the coverage for the combined space. The [360 HIT (61.2 m2, RM50/mo)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) works for smaller open-plan areas under 60 m2.

## Common placement mistakes

**Behind the sofa.** This blocks the air intake and creates a dead zone where the purifier recycles the same trapped air.

**Inside a cabinet or shelf unit.** Some homeowners hide the purifier inside furniture for aesthetics. This blocks intake and exhaust, reducing performance by 50% or more.

**On the floor in a corner.** Corners restrict airflow from at least two sides. Move the unit at least 50 cm away from any corner.

**Next to the aircond indoor unit.** Placing the purifier directly below the aircond indoor unit means it is cleaning air that has already been pushed past the aircond's mesh filter — but the aircond's strong airflow can also blow particles past the purifier's intake before they are captured. Offset by at least 1 metre.

**On a high shelf.** Most floor-standing purifiers are designed to draw air from the lower half of the room (where particles settle) and push clean air upward. Elevating the unit on a shelf reverses this cycle. Keep floor-standing models on the floor.

## One purifier or multiple

If your budget allows, placing one purifier in the living room and one in the master bedroom is the most effective setup for a Malaysian home. The living room unit handles daytime cooking fumes and haze infiltration. The bedroom unit runs overnight for clean sleeping air.

If you have one unit, move it to the bedroom at night and back to the living room in the morning. Most LG PuriCare models are light enough to reposition easily.

## Related reading

- [Air Purifier for Asthma and Allergies](/blog/air-purifier-asthma-allergies-malaysia/)
- [Air Purifier vs Aircond: Do You Need Both?](/blog/air-purifier-vs-aircond-do-you-need-both/)

title: Air Purifier for Open-Plan Kitchens
description: How to tackle cooking odour in Malaysian open-plan homes with an air purifier. Models with activated carbon for wok fumes, sambal and frying. From RM50/mo.
excerpt: Open-plan kitchens are popular in Malaysian condos and new homes, but cooking odour from sambal, frying and wok hei travels straight to the sofa. Here is how an air purifier with activated carbon helps.
body:
Open-plan living is now the default layout in Malaysian condos and many new landed homes. The kitchen, dining and living areas flow into one space without walls. It looks great in the developer's showroom, but the moment you fry ikan bilis or cook sambal belacan, the entire floor smells like a hawker stall. An air purifier with the right filter combination can reduce cooking odour and airborne grease particles — if you choose the right model and place it correctly.

## Why open-plan kitchens are an air quality challenge

In a traditional walled kitchen, cooking fumes are contained. The exhaust hood captures most of the smoke and grease, and the kitchen door keeps the rest from reaching the living room. In an open-plan layout, there is no barrier. Cooking fumes travel freely across the entire space.

Malaysian cooking makes this worse than it sounds. Wok cooking at high heat produces fine oil droplets (0.5-5 microns) and volatile organic compounds (the gaseous molecules that carry the smell). Deep-frying generates a persistent grease particulate that settles on soft furnishings. Sambal, shrimp paste and curry pastes release pungent sulphur and amine compounds that linger for hours.

A range hood helps — but even the most powerful range hood captures only 50-70% of cooking fumes when used correctly. The rest disperses into the open-plan space.

## What an air purifier can and cannot do for cooking odour

An air purifier addresses cooking fumes in two ways.

**HEPA filter for particles.** The H13 HEPA filter captures fine oil droplets, smoke particles and grease particulates. These are the visible haze that forms above the stove and drifts across the room. A properly sized purifier can clear these particles from a room within 20-30 minutes after cooking.

**Activated carbon for gases and odours.** The gaseous compounds that carry cooking smells are not captured by HEPA — they pass through the fibre media as gas. An activated carbon or deodorising filter adsorbs these gas molecules onto its porous surface. The more carbon the filter contains, the more odour it can neutralise before it needs replacement.

An air purifier will not eliminate cooking odour instantly. It reduces the concentration and duration. Instead of smelling sambal for 4 hours, you might smell it for 30-45 minutes. The difference is noticeable and practical, but do not expect the purifier to make your home odourless while the wok is still on the flame.

## Which LG PuriCare models handle cooking odour

Every LG PuriCare air purifier includes some form of deodorising filter alongside the H13 HEPA. However, the carbon density and filter design vary across models.

| Model | Filter setup | Coverage | Deodorising layer | From |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) | H13 HEPA + 3-stage deodorising | 61.2 m2 | Yes (dedicated layer) | RM50/mo |
| [AeroBooster](/products/air-purifiers/aero-booster) | Dual H13 HEPA + UVnano | 52.8 m2 | Yes (carbon layer) | RM65/mo |
| [Alpha Pet Single](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-single) | V-Pet H13 HEPA | 61.2 m2 | Yes (enhanced for organic compounds) | RM75/mo |
| [Alpha Pet Double](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) | 2x V-Pet H13 HEPA | 104 m2 | Yes (dual deodorising layers) | RM105/mo |

The [360 HIT](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit) is our most recommended model for open-plan kitchens at the budget level. Its three-stage deodorising filter is specifically designed to handle household odours including cooking fumes, and the 61.2 m2 coverage is sufficient for most Malaysian open-plan ground floors.

[![LG PuriCare 360 HIT — 3-stage deodorising filter for cooking fumes](/uploads/products/puricare-360-hit/main.avif)](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-hit)

For larger open-plan spaces (above 60 m2), the [Alpha Pet Double at RM105/month](/products/air-purifiers/puricare-360-alpha-pet-double) provides 104 m2 coverage with dual deodorising layers. The name says "Pet" but the enhanced carbon filtration works equally well on cooking odour — the organic compound adsorption mechanism is the same.

## How to position the purifier in an open-plan kitchen

**Place it between the kitchen and the living area.** This intercepts cooking fumes before they reach your sofa and soft furnishings. The purifier acts as an air barrier between the cooking zone and the relaxation zone.

**Keep it at least 2 metres from the stove.** Proximity to high heat and steam can damage the filter and electronics. Grease splatter near the cooking surface is a risk to the intake mesh.

**Do not rely on the purifier alone.** Use the range hood during cooking. The range hood captures the bulk of the fumes at the source, and the air purifier handles what escapes. Together, they reduce odour duration by 60-80% compared to using neither.

**Run the purifier on high during and for 30 minutes after cooking.** Most LG PuriCare models will detect the spike in airborne particles and VOCs automatically, ramping up the fan speed. If yours has a manual turbo mode, use it during active cooking for faster fume clearance.

**Close the bedroom doors during cooking.** This prevents cooking fumes from entering bedrooms. After cooking, open the doors briefly and let the purifier do its work in the main space.

## Tips to reduce cooking odour at the source

An air purifier helps, but prevention at the source reduces the load on the filter and extends its lifespan.

**Use the range hood on maximum during cooking.** Turn it on 2 minutes before cooking and leave it on for 10 minutes after you turn off the flame. Most Malaysian households turn the hood off too early.

**Close the kitchen window during frying.** This sounds counterintuitive, but an open window behind the stove creates a cross-draught that pulls fumes past the range hood and into the living area. Keep the window closed and let the hood do its job.

**Wipe down the stove and splashback immediately after cooking.** Grease residue on surfaces re-releases VOCs as it oxidises over the next few hours. A quick wipe with dish soap removes the source.

**Ventilate after cooking, not during.** Once cooking is done, open windows for 10-15 minutes to flush the air. Then close them and let the purifier maintain clean air for the rest of the day.

## Carbon filter lifespan in high-cooking households

Activated carbon filters have a finite adsorption capacity. In a household that cooks Malaysian-style twice daily (morning and evening), the carbon layer will saturate faster than in a household that cooks once a day.

On LG Subscribe plans, filter replacements are included on schedule. If the LG ThinQ app indicates the filter needs replacement before the scheduled date, contact the LG Subscribe team and they will arrange an early replacement at no extra cost. Do not run a saturated carbon filter — once the carbon is full, it stops adsorbing odour and the purifier only captures particles.

## The realistic expectation

An air purifier in an open-plan kitchen will not make your home smell like an air-conditioned shopping mall during cooking. What it will do is reduce the intensity and duration of cooking odour significantly, prevent grease particles from settling on your sofa and curtains, and bring air quality back to clean levels within 30-45 minutes after cooking.

Combined with a good range hood and basic kitchen hygiene, this is enough for most families to enjoy open-plan living without the air quality compromise.
